## Coordination-session peer review (owner-directed collaboration)

Owner reviewed and CONFIRMED: off-purity fix eb61267 sound; injection reproduction T1-T4 matches PR claims; all12_fixed.json triad coherent. Listed 6 discrepancies to resolve (verified against FLITS origin/main 17d9d26): (1) SELF-INCONSISTENT MAIN — merged campaign results (commit ae67bdf, `analysis/window-tuning-campaign-2026-07-17/results/campaign_results.jsonl`) generated with PRE-fix windows (zach off_snr=4.97 α=2.53 artifact FAIL, chromatica α=0.40, freya α=−0.99, all science_status=diagnostic_only); campaign must be RERUN through fixed selector, status upgrades earned by official pipeline; (2) commit harness + 2L table into `analysis/<campaign-dir>/`; (3) confirm grid_regularization not bypassed, regenerate all12 through config path; (4) run injection suite for burst_core vs burst_lims, adopt reproducible winner, document in README; (5) add per-subband physicality reject to ladder plots as annotated rejections not silent drops; (6) one FLITS PR = harness + 2L table + rerun results + ladder figures + README, owner reviews line-by-line then handles Faber2026 pin bump + evidence-ledger + manuscript rewrite (results.tex L285-302 still claims zero CHIME detections). Owner: do NOT touch Faber2026 manuscript or shared main checkout.

Agent replied: agreed on process; corrected owner's item 3 (grid-reg NOT bypassed — verified); found the rerun DOES change the triad materially but via item 4 (estimator), not item 3. Ran official campaign driver on chromatica+zach: driver's `chosen`=matched-weight-tail gives chromatica α=0.51 (n=3), zach α=1.83 (n=4), NOT the burst_core triad (chromatica 1.66, zach 2.05). Extended harness (`inject_estimator_suite.py` then `inject_discriminate.py`): on clean data all three estimators agree ~2%; when scattering tail carries broad structure distinct from core scintle (γ_n=0.08, γ_b=2.0), **burst_core recovers unbiased (0.83×), tail-expanded inflates 2.6-3.6×, matched-weight-tail inflates 1.3-1.9×**. Decision: burst_core wins, settled reproducibly.

Patched driver `run_window_campaign.py` in mirror: `chosen` estimator changed from matched-weight tail-expanded to `core-boxcar` (unweighted burst_core); matched-weight-tail + tail-expanded kept as variants. Hit JSON serialization error (raw weights ndarray in variant dict) — fixed by stripping raw weights from serialized windows, keying dedup on (burst,off,weighted), passing weights via time_weights in variant refit. **Official 12-burst rerun** (`campaign_rerun_v2`): chromatica α=+2.92±1.34 (n=2, NOT the standalone 1.66), zach α=+1.99±0.48 (n=3, stable), freya α=−0.79 (n=2), hamilton α=+8.94 (n=2). Diagnosed the chromatica discrepancy: official driver builds profile on validity-span-sliced spectrum → picks off=[255,369] (post-burst, off_snr=2.93), while standalone picks off=[0,131] (pre-burst, off_snr=5.92); same burst_core [185,214]. Off-window sensitivity sweep: **pre-burst off-windows all give stable α≈+1.7 to +1.9 n=4** (0,131→1.66; 20,175→1.79; 0,175→1.85), post-burst give n=2 with top subband railing γ=0.007-0.010 (post-burst region is the scattering tail's home, contaminates top-subband de-scalloping). Agent's emerging conclusion: off-window rule should PREFER pre-burst clean region; the sliced-profile purity metric got chromatica backwards.

A pre-burst-preference test (`inject`/refit loop over chromatica/zach/whitney/freya classifying pre vs post free runs) was dispatched and **left running in the background** (exec_id `ae9d66b9-736b-4622-867f-47d46613009a`) when the two-band directive arrived — its output not yet seen at chunk end.

## NEW owner directive at chunk end (two-band joint α) — NOT yet started

Owner extended the deliverable: fit scintillation scaling index α JOINTLY across both instruments, 400 MHz (CHIME bottom) → 1530 MHz (DSA top), not intra-CHIME only. Scope: every burst with both-band measurements — CHIME triad (zach, chromatica, freya; hamilton diagnostic-only) × DSA-band Lorentzian components (July-7 catalog, only oran certified, rest provisional). Note `analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/prototype_two_band_event.py` prototypes a two-band event — build on or supersede explicitly. Required: (1) TWO-SCREEN AMBIGUITY — run one-screen consistency test per burst before quoting joint α; where it fails report per-band αs + two-screen decomposition; (2) PER-BAND SYSTEMATICS — CHIME from config-path rerun (grid-reg ON), DSA July-7 provisional, propagate asymmetry into joint-fit errors; (3) RESOLUTION FLOORS — check each band's min resolvable Δν_d against α-extrapolated prediction for consistent censoring; (4) this joint fit + per-burst figure REPLACES the DSA-only summary (fig:dsa_scint_gamma): one panel per burst, both bands' points, joint/per-band power-law overlay, censored bands as limits. Fold into same FLITS PR. Owner wants reply with: two-band data availability matrix, one-screen test design, whether DSA July-7 provisional blocks any triad. **The 400-1530 MHz joint α for the triad becomes the headline manuscript number — highest validation bar in the PR.** Not yet acted upon.
